questions.question: Define Software Project Management and list its six key constraints.
keyPoints: Definition: specialized discipline for planning, executing, monitoring, controlling software projects
Six constraints: Scope, Time, Cost, Quality, Resources, Risk
Balancing these constraints is the core challenge

questions.question: Explain why software projects are considered more challenging than traditional engineering projects.
keyPoints: Product is intangible - difficult to visualize progress
Requirements often change during development
Innovation uncertainty - no standard process
Technology evolution - rapid changes
Complex human interactions and stakeholder coordination

questions.question: What is the 'Triple Constraint' or 'Iron Triangle' in project management? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Three constraints: Scope, Time, Cost
Interdependent relationship - changing one affects others
Example: Reducing deadline (Time) requires either increased budget (Cost) or reduced features (Scope)
Quality is often considered the fourth dimension

questions.question: Describe the key responsibilities and essential skills of a Software Project Manager.
keyPoints: Responsibilities: coordinate resources, manage timelines/budgets, ensure quality, mitigate risks, communicate with stakeholders
Essential skills: leadership, communication, problem-solving, risk assessment, time management, negotiation, adaptability
Technical understanding of software development

questions.question: Differentiate between a project and a program in software project management.
keyPoints: Project: temporary endeavor with unique deliverables, definite start/end
Program: group of related projects managed coordinately
Program achieves benefits not available from individual projects
Program aligns with organizational strategy

questions.question: What is stakeholder management? Why is it important in software projects?
keyPoints: Process of identifying, analyzing, engaging parties with interest in project
Includes: customers, users, team, management, external parties
Ensures needs are met and expectations managed
Critical for project success due to multiple competing interests

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'scope creep' in software projects and suggest how it can be prevented.
keyPoints: Scope creep: uncontrolled expansion of project scope
Without adjusting time, cost, or resources
Prevention: clearly defined requirements, formal change control process, regular scope reviews
stakeholder sign-offs on deliverables

questions.question: Discuss the importance of Software Project Management in software engineering. Explain the key constraints and how they impact project success. Include a practical example to illustrate.
keyPoints: SPM ensures projects delivered on time, within budget, meeting quality
Manages complexity and reduces failure rates
Optimizes resource utilization
Key constraints: Scope, Time, Cost, Quality, Resources, Risk (STAGRR)
Triple constraint: Scope-Time-Cost relationship
Practical example: Mobile app development with deadline - must balance features vs time

questions.question: Compare traditional project management with software project management. What unique challenges does a software project manager face?
keyPoints: Traditional PM: tangible products, established processes, stable requirements
Software PM: intangible products, changing requirements, innovation uncertainty
Unique challenges: product invisibility, no standard process, technology evolution, complex stakeholder management
Need for continuous adaptation
More emphasis on risk management and communication

questions.question: A software company has been experiencing project failures. As a consultant, suggest how implementing proper project management practices can help. Discuss the role of the project manager and key knowledge areas.
keyPoints: Proper SPM practices ensure structured approach
Role of PM: planning, coordinating, controlling, communication, risk management
Key knowledge areas: Integration, Scope, Schedule, Cost, Quality, Resource, Communication, Risk, Stakeholder, Procurement
Benefits: better estimation, risk mitigation, stakeholder alignment, quality assurance
